raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need command: find /system -type f -exec grep -n -w 'foo' {} + | sed 's/^/FILE: /'? Actually need precede found string with its number in file. So output line number and string. Use grep -n -w. So command: find /system -type f -exec grep -n -w 'foo' {} +. That prints file:line:foo. That's fine.

So output line number and string. Use grep -n -w. So command: find /system -type f -exec grep -n -w 'foo' {} +. That prints file:line:foo. That's fine.
